name: fastapi-templates
description: Create production-ready FastAPI projects with async patterns, dependency injection, and comprehensive error handling. Use when building new FastAPI applications or setting up backend API projects.
capabilities: Generate complete FastAPI project structures with proper layering
Implement async database operations with SQLAlchemy
Create JWT authentication with dependency injection
Build repository and service layer patterns
Generate comprehensive test fixtures and examples
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 32 patterns, but all are false positives. This is a documentation skill containing markdown code examples and templates, not executable code. External command detections are markdown backtick code blocks. Network findings reference test mocks. Environment access patterns show standard configuration examples. No actual security risks exist.
valueStatement: Developers need production-ready FastAPI project structures without starting from scratch. This skill provides complete templates with async patterns, dependency injection, and layered architecture for high-performance APIs.
userTitle: Build FastAPI projects with async patterns
limitations: Templates require manual customization for specific business logic
Does not deploy or run the generated code
Requires Python 3.8+ and FastAPI installed in target environment
Database migrations must be configured separately

useCases.title: Startup API Development
useCases.description: Quickly scaffold a new FastAPI project with authentication, database integration, and proper project structure for MVP development.

useCases.title: Microservice Template
useCases.description: Generate consistent API structures across multiple microservices with standardized error handling and dependency injection patterns.

useCases.title: Learning Resource
useCases.description: Study production-ready patterns for async operations, repository pattern, and layered architecture in modern Python APIs.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Project Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new FastAPI project with user authentication, PostgreSQL database, and basic CRUD endpoints for a resource.

promptTemplates.title: Add Authentication Layer
promptTemplates.prompt: Add JWT-based authentication to my existing FastAPI project with token refresh and password reset functionality.

promptTemplates.title: Implement Repository Pattern
promptTemplates.prompt: Refactor my FastAPI routes to use repository and service layers with async database operations and proper dependency injection.

promptTemplates.title: Complete Microservice Template
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a production-ready microservice with FastAPI including health checks, structured logging, error handling middleware, and Docker configuration.

bestPractices: Use async database operations throughout to avoid blocking the event loop
Separate concerns with repository, service, and API layers for testability
Validate all inputs with Pydantic schemas and handle errors consistently
antiPatterns: Using synchronous database drivers in async route handlers
Placing business logic directly in route handlers instead of service layer
Accessing database directly from routes without repository abstraction

faq.question: What Python version is required?
faq.answer: Python 3.8 or higher is required for full async/await support and type hint features used in the templates.

faq.question: Can I use this with MySQL instead of PostgreSQL?
faq.answer: Yes, the templates use SQLAlchemy which supports multiple databases. Change the DATABASE_URL and install the appropriate async driver.

faq.question: Does this skill deploy my application?
faq.answer: No, this skill generates project templates and code. Deployment must be configured separately using your preferred platform.

faq.question: Are the authentication templates production-ready?
faq.answer: The JWT authentication follows security best practices, but you should review SECRET_KEY management and add rate limiting for production.

faq.question: Can I customize the generated project structure?
faq.answer: Yes, the templates are starting points. Modify any generated files to match your specific requirements and architecture preferences.

faq.question: What testing framework do the templates use?
faq.answer: The templates use pytest with pytest-asyncio for async test support and httpx for API endpoint testing.
